flashcards.definition: When a region uses more than 25% of its available renewable fresh water supply; affecting ~2 billion people.
Removing salt from seawater to produce fresh water; energy-intensive but increasingly important in arid regions.
The hidden water embedded in producing goods (e.g., ~15,000 liters to produce 1 kg of beef).
Total volume of fresh water used to produce goods and services consumed by an individual or community.
Ratio of water used by crops to water applied; drip irrigation (~90%) vs. flood irrigation (~50%).
